input.scenario_description: A database server crashed at 3:00 AM. No one was logged in. Hypothesis A: A rare cosmic ray flipped a bit in the RAM causing a cascade failure. Hypothesis B: The automated maintenance script ran out of memory due to a memory leak.

output.logic_rationale: In Forensic mode, we prioritize likelihood. Cosmic rays causing crashes are statistically extremely rare (high complexity/low probability). A memory leak in a maintenance script is a common software error. Hypothesis B requires fewer assumptions regarding external physical anomalies.

input.scenario_description: Why do objects fall? Hypothesis A: Invisible intangible angels push them down. Hypothesis B: Curvature of spacetime caused by mass (General Relativity).

output.logic_rationale: In Academic mode, we look for ontological economy. Hypothesis A invents a new category of entity (angels) with specific, untestable properties. Hypothesis B uses existing frameworks (mass, space) to explain the phenomenon. While GR is mathematically complex, it is ontologically parsimonious compared to inventing supernatural agents.
